evals.prompt: I keep running out of disk space on my dev box. Write me a bash script called cleanup-logs that takes a directory and a number of days as arguments, and deletes all *.log files under that directory older than that many days. I want to see which files it deleted.
evals.expected_output: An executable bash script cleanup-logs that validates its two arguments, deletes old .log files with find, and prints what it deleted.
expectations: The script file exists and is executable (test -x passes)
The first line is exactly '#!/usr/bin/env bash'
The script contains 'set -euo pipefail'
bash -n accepts the script (valid syntax)
shellcheck reports no issues (exit code 0)
Running the script with no arguments exits non-zero and prints usage to stderr
The script deletes only *.log files older than the given number of days (find with -mtime or equivalent)
The script prints the files it deleted
The script has no unnecessary comments or decorative echo output (at most 2 comment lines beyond the shebang)

evals.prompt: need a script to back up my ~/notes folder into ~/backups as a dated tar.gz (like notes-2026-07-06.tar.gz) and keep only the 5 newest backups. call it backup-notes
evals.expected_output: An executable bash script backup-notes that creates a date-stamped tar.gz of ~/notes in ~/backups and prunes all but the 5 newest archives.
expectations: The script file exists and is executable (test -x passes)
The first line is exactly '#!/usr/bin/env bash'
The script contains 'set -euo pipefail'
bash -n accepts the script (valid syntax)
shellcheck reports no issues (exit code 0)
The archive filename embeds the current date (e.g. via date +%F)
The script removes older backups so only the 5 newest remain
The script does not pre-check for installed commands like tar (failure is obvious on use)
The script has no unnecessary comments or decorative echo output (at most 2 comment lines beyond the shebang)

evals.prompt: write a lil bash helper git-prune-merged that deletes local branches already merged into main, but never main/master or whatever branch im currently on
evals.expected_output: An executable bash script git-prune-merged that deletes local branches merged into main while protecting main, master, and the current branch.
expectations: The script file exists and is executable (test -x passes)
The first line is exactly '#!/usr/bin/env bash'
The script contains 'set -euo pipefail'
bash -n accepts the script (valid syntax)
shellcheck reports no issues (exit code 0)
The script excludes main, master, and the current branch from deletion
In a test git repo with one merged and one unmerged branch, the script deletes only the merged branch
The script has no unnecessary comments or decorative echo output (at most 2 comment lines beyond the shebang)
